fills.note: Ruth pericope → standard BCD ref per convention.

fills.value_json: {"_note":"Scenes 2-3 shift to INTIMATE (the whispered exchange in the dark at midnight); one moment at 3:10 lifts to CEREMONIAL (Boaz's blessing form). The v.13 chai-YHWH oath stays INTIMATE per the P03 precedent. Scene 1 is the narrator's plain INFORMAL_CASUAL telling.","scene_level":[{"scene_id":"S2","override_value":"INTIMATE"},{"scene_id":"S3","override_value":"INTIMATE"}],"moment_level":[{"verse":"3:10a","override_value":"CEREMONIAL"}]}
fills.note: MM Section 1 multi-level register tagging encoded structurally. Quoted oath at v.13 deliberately NOT lifted to CEREMONIAL per the map's own note.

Movement: execute Naomi's plan into the dark → midnight startle/recognition → the wing petition spoken past the plan → blessing → the nearer-redeemer complication → oath seal. Three mints required (no approved fit for plan-execution, the kanaf marriage-petition, or the legal-queue disclosure).

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM 2.1/3F-S1: Ruth 'does all her mother-in-law commanded' — the carrying-out of P08's plan; no approved arc token names execution of a prior plan.
MM 3:9: 'spread your wing over your servant' — the kanaf marriage/protection petition closing the 2:12 image; no approved arc token covers this petition.
MM 3:12: 'there is a redeemer nearer than I' turns 2:20's comfort into a legal queue; distinct beat with no approved fit.

fills.note: Threshing floor/gate (physical); redeemer & mother/daughter-in-law (kinship); kinsman-redeemer law & gate-legal venue (institutional); the heavy carry-forward of P08's plan, 2:12 kanaf, 2:20 redeemer, 2:1 chayil, 1:8/2:20 hesed (prior-pericope); YHWH invoked in blessing and oath (divine); the wordplays the hearer is expected to catch (audience-knowledge).

Quiet motion told plainly/economically; the whispered intimacy of the exchange; the held breath between 'you are a redeemer' and 'nearer than I' (anticipatory); the strange calm of the settled protocol (stilled).
Scene 1 told fast (brisk); the midnight startle (rises); the talk unrolling slowly (slowed); the held breath (holds); the settled protocol close (settles).
Advances the plot to its hinge; closes the 2:12 wing-image as petition; reactivates the 3:5 assent-pattern in Boaz's mouth; opens the legal redeemer-queue; stages the gate scene.
Ruth acting on Naomi's plan; relational role carried, executing party.
Narrator uses 'Ruth' — unmarked.
Boaz the household's redeemer-kinsman, asleep by his grain; he does not yet know she is there.
Narrator names him 'Boaz' — unmarked.
Naomi the absent author of the plan Ruth carries out.

Narrator refers to Naomi only as 'her mother-in-law' (חֲמוֹתָהּ); left unmarked to avoid over-marking a passing kinship reference — flag for reviewer if the chamot relation should be preserved as marked.
קְצֵה הָעֲרֵמָה / 'the end of the grain heap' — not in the registry; descriptive PL code proposed (reviewer's call on canonical form).
Silent staging: descent to the floor, the meal, lying down, the soft approach and uncovering. No approved scene_kind names this nocturnal silent-approach staging; mint proposed.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM 3F-S1: 'Executes the plan to the letter and sets the night's stage' — a wordless nighttime approach scene with no approved fit (MEAL_SCENE/INSTRUCTION_SCENE miss the staging focus).

fills.note: Boaz the startled sleeper; still the household redeemer-kin in function.
MM explicitly marks: 'the man' (הָאִישׁ) at v.8 — name withheld until his answer.
Ruth as petitioner; relational role carried.
MM marks the self-naming 'Ruth your servant' (amah) — the marriageable-servant word, a step up from shifchah of 2:13 (FIG_0132).
B18 is the redeemer-role Ruth names Boaz into — the ground of her request.
'a redeemer' (goel) is the role-naming, carried in objects; not a marked naming of a person.
בַּחֲצִי הַלַּיְלָה / 'at half of the night' — no registry TM code; descriptive TM proposed (L3 registry proposal).
The midnight recognition and Ruth's petition for the wing — an appeal scene.
Append: Elimelech's line is invoked (the redeemer duty of B2's line, MM 3A-B18 relationship) and Naomi as Ruth's mother-in-law. Both REFERENCED.
Boaz blesses, pledges, discloses, swears — the household redeemer (and not the nearest).
Named 'Boaz' — unmarked.
Ruth blessed and praised; relational role carried.
MM marks Boaz's twice-used address 'my daughter' (בִּתִּי) — the same address Naomi uses; _INTIMATE idiom per worked pair.
YHWH invoked in the blessing and sworn by in the oath.
'YHWH' — unmarked.
The nearer redeemer — prior claim in the legal queue.
MM marks: 'a redeemer nearer than I' — name withheld here (resolved at the gate, 4:1).
'all the gate of my people' — the town community whose knowledge of Ruth's worth Boaz cites.
Collective 'all the gate of my people' carried in role/place; not marked here.
The young men (habachurim) Ruth did not go after — the measure of her last hesed. No approved role token for unchosen suitors; mint proposed.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM 3A-S3: 'the young men of marrying age, poor or rich; none of them chosen' — their scene function is the unchosen marital alternative, with no approved role_in_scene_being fit.

fills.note: Surface form הַבַּחוּרִים / 'the young men'; carried in role, not a marked personal naming.
The young men (הַבַּחוּרִים) are a referenced group with no registry code; deliberately uncoded per the B? convention. Reviewer may register a B code if desired.
הַלַּיְלָה … בַבֹּקֶר / 'the night … in the morning' — the protocol's two times; no registry TM code, descriptive TM proposed.

Boaz's answer opens with the blessing (CEREMONIAL lift) and carries pledge, disclosure, and oath. BLESSING_SCENE chosen as the dominant opening move; the oath-sealing also runs here (cf. VOW_SCENE) — flag for reviewer if a combined kind is preferred.
Append: Elimelech's line invoked via the nearer-redeemer's kinship (MM 3A-S3 B19 relationship).
Ruth's descent to the floor with compliance to the command. No approved kind for descent/compliance; mint proposed.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM P1: 'she went down (yarad) to the threshing floor and did all her mother-in-law commanded' — a descent-and-compliance event with no approved proposition_kind fit.

fills.note: Compliance with Naomi's P08 plan preserved as compliance_form.
Reuse of approved kind.
FIG_0141 heart-good-after-meal carried as resulting_disposition; drank kept as boolean to avoid an axis mint.
No approved kind for lying down; mint proposed.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM P3: 'he came to lie down at the end of the grain heap' — a lying-down event with no approved proposition_kind fit.

fills.note: Lies down after eating and being content.
Ruth's soft approach, uncovering, and lying down — three actions in one MM proposition; decomposed into components. Mint for the kind.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM P4: 'she came softly, uncovered the place of his feet, and lay down' — a multi-action approach with no approved proposition_kind fit.

fills.note: Three component actions. WALKED reused for 'came softly'; UNCOVERED_FEET and LAY_DOWN are action-axis mints (declared).

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM P4 / CB_0042 margelot: 'uncovered the place of his feet' — the plan's central act; no approved action token covers uncovering.
MM P4: 'and lay down' — a lying-down action with no approved action-axis fit.

fills.note: The midnight startle (FIG_0130 Vayyecherad). No approved kind; mint proposed.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM P5: 'the man trembled and twisted' — the vayyecherad startle, with no approved proposition_kind fit.

fills.note: 'the man' naming preserved at the startle.
The presence at his feet causes the startle.
Reuse of approved kind.
'and behold (hinneh) a woman' — he perceives her only as 'a woman', not yet Ruth.
Reuse of approved kind.
'who are you?' — identity-seeking question.
Ruth's self-identifying answer.
amah self-naming preserved (FIG_0132).
Answers the question P7.
Reuse — the wing petition.
The kanaf petition closing 2:12. Imperative-form humble petition → DIRECTS_HEARER_TO_DO (the closest closed speech act); note the register is a petition, not a command.
Ruth states the ground of her petition — the redeemer-word in her own mouth for the first time.
First occurrence of the goel word in Ruth's speech (the plan withheld it).
The ground of the petition P9.
Reuse — Boaz's blessing (CEREMONIAL lift).
Worked-pair blessing idiom. WISHES_FOR_HEARER is the closest closed speech act for the blessing form (no BLESSES act exists).
Boaz's answer responds to the petition.
Boaz praises her last hesed.
Hesed-thread third station (1:8, 2:20, 3:10); FIG_0133/FIG_0111.
The content of the hesed-praise: not going after the young men.
Young men carried as B? (uncoded group).
Explains the hesed of P12.
'do not fear' + the pledge 'all that you say I will do' — reassurance. No approved kind; mint proposed.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM P14: 'do not fear; all that you say I will do for you' — a reassurance/pledge act (FIG_0123/FIG_0136) with no approved proposition_kind fit.

fills.note: The pledge echoes Ruth's 3:5 assent in reverse (FIG_0136); VOWS used for the binding pledge — the formal chai-YHWH oath is distinct at P20.
Answers her petition with reassurance and pledge.
The reason for the pledge: the gate knows her worth.
eshet chayil completes the 2:1 pair (FIG_0134); the gate named as the venue the legal scene will use.
Gives the reason for the pledge in P14.
Boaz owns the redeemer role.
'truly I am a redeemer'.
The nearer-redeemer disclosure.
Turns 2:20's nearness into a legal queue (FIG_0138/FIG_0112); resolved at the gate (4:1-8).
The 'but' complication against P16's ownership.
'lodge the night' — a directive.
Command encoded per the action-idiom ruling: DIRECTED + commanded_step.
The disclosure necessitates the night's protocol.
The conditional morning protocol (FIG_0140 reverse-handoff); contingent plan stated, decomposed into components.
redeem-upon-redeem; the self-pledge half carries VOWS (sealed by the oath at P20).
The protocol follows from the nearer-redeemer disclosure.
The chai-YHWH oath.
FIG_0135 chai-YHWH formula; INTIMATE register per the map (not lifted to ceremonial).
Seals the protocol of P19.
'lie down until the morning' — closing directive (FIG_0139 lie-down authority-transfer).
Command encoded per the action-idiom ruling.
Closes after the oath; last proposition.

Propositions kept at meaning-map granularity. P4 (came softly / uncovered feet / lay down) and P19 (the conditional morning protocol) carry internal components inside event_specific_slots rather than separate L3 rows; no further decomposition needed. Flags re-anchored by verse already match the skeleton.

remarks: Register: pericope stays INFORMAL_CASUAL (NARRATIVE); S2/S3 INTIMATE and the 3:10 blessing CEREMONIAL encoded as overrides; v.13 oath deliberately left INTIMATE per the map. Mints declared: arc PLAN_EXECUTION/WING_PETITION/NEARER_REDEEMER_DISCLOSURE; scene_kind NIGHT_APPROACH_SCENE; role POTENTIAL_SUITORS; proposition_kind WENT_DOWN/LAY_DOWN/APPROACHED/TREMBLED/REASSURED; action UNCOVERED_FEET/LAY_DOWN — this floor scene's physical verbs are largely outside the Ruth-derived enumeration. Proposed registry IDs (reviewer's call): PL_END_OF_GRAIN_HEAP, TM_MIDDLE_OF_THE_NIGHT, TM_NIGHT_UNTIL_MORNING; the young men carried as B? (uncoded group). Two speech-act forcings flagged in-note: the kanaf petition (P9) and Boaz's blessing (P11) have no exact closed-list act — DIRECTS_HEARER_TO_DO and WISHES_FOR_HEARER chosen as nearest. S3 scene_kind set BLESSING_SCENE but the oath-sealing also runs there; flag if a combined kind is wanted.
